description: Ferrospice Simulation Engine - SPICE circuit simulator library in Rust, rewriting ngspice. Done when all 113 ngspice base test suite .cir files pass.

userStories.title: Create ferrospice-core crate with sparse matrix types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a sparse matrix representation suitable for MNA so that circuit equations can be assembled and solved.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate ferrospice-core in the workspace
Sparse matrix struct supporting dynamic insertion and triplet-form assembly
LU factorization and solve (use faer or similar — pick what fits, pure Rust preferred)
Unit tests: assemble a 3x3 system, solve it, verify result within 1e-12 tolerance

userStories.title: MNA matrix assembly from netlist
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to convert a parsed Netlist into an MNA equation system so that the circuit can be solved.
acceptanceCriteria: Function that takes a ferrospice_netlist::Netlist and produces an MNA system (matrix + RHS vector)
Node name to matrix index mapping (ground node 0 is the reference, not in matrix)
Stamps for: resistor, independent voltage source (adds branch equation), independent current source
Unit test: voltage divider (V1=5V, R1=1k, R2=1k) produces MNA system with correct dimensions and entries

userStories.title: DC operating point solver (.op)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DC operating point solver so that node voltages and branch currents can be computed for a DC circuit.
acceptanceCriteria: simulate_op(netlist: &Netlist) -> SimResult function in the public API
Returns node voltages and voltage source branch currents
Test: voltage divider V1=5V, R1=1k, R2=1k produces V(mid) = 2.5V within 1e-9
Test: series resistors with current source produce correct node voltages
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/resistance/res_simple.cir as a Rust integration test

userStories.title: Capacitor and inductor device stamps
userStories.description: As a developer, I need C and L device stamps so that reactive elements are represented in the MNA system (for DC: C=open, L=short).
acceptanceCriteria: Capacitor stamp: open circuit in DC (no contribution to DC matrix)
Inductor stamp: short circuit in DC (zero-resistance branch equation)
Test: RC circuit DC op — capacitor acts as open, voltage across C equals source voltage through divider
Test: RL circuit DC op — inductor acts as short, full current flows

userStories.title: Port ngspice resistance test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ngspice resistance tests running against ferrospice to validate basic DC solving.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test module that loads .cir files from ngspice-upstream/tests/resistance/
Parse each .cir with ferrospice_netlist, simulate with ferrospice, compare output to .out
Output comparison uses the same filtering as ngspice check.sh (ignore metadata, compare numerical values)
All 3 resistance tests pass (skip temp-dependent ones with #[ignore] and a TODO if needed)

userStories.title: DC sweep analysis (.dc)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DC sweep analysis so that a source can be swept across a range and the operating point computed at each step.
acceptanceCriteria: .dc src start stop step sweeps the named source and computes OP at each point
Results returned as SimResult with one data point per sweep step
Test: sweep V1 from 0 to 5V in 1V steps across a resistor produces 6 data points with correct I(V1)

userStories.title: Newton-Raphson nonlinear solver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Newton-Raphson iteration loop so that nonlinear devices (diodes, transistors) can be solved.
acceptanceCriteria: NR iteration with configurable convergence tolerance (ABSTOL, RELTOL, VNTOL matching ngspice defaults)
Convergence check: both absolute and relative criteria on node voltages and branch currents
Source stepping or Gmin stepping fallback when NR fails to converge
Test: solve a simple nonlinear equation (e.g., diode I-V) via NR, verify convergence in < 20 iterations

userStories.title: Diode device model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a diode device model so that nonlinear circuits with PN junctions can be simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: .model parameters: IS, N, RS, BV (breakdown), CJO (junction capacitance for later)
NR stamp: companion model (conductance + current source) linearized at each iteration
Test: diode with 1V source and 1k resistor produces forward voltage ~0.6-0.7V
Test: diode I-V curve via .dc sweep matches ngspice within 1e-6 relative tolerance

userStories.title: Transient analysis engine (.tran)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need transient (time-domain) analysis so that circuits with time-varying sources and reactive elements can be simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: Backward Euler integration method (simplest, matches ngspice default starter)
Trapezoidal integration method (ngspice default after startup)
Capacitor companion model: stamp updates each timestep based on integration method
Inductor companion model: stamp updates each timestep based on integration method
Test: RC step response — charge curve matches analytical V(t) = V0*(1-exp(-t/RC)) within 1% at 5 time constants
Test: LC oscillator — frequency matches 1/(2*pi*sqrt(LC)) within 1%

userStories.title: Time-varying source waveforms
userStories.description: As a developer, I need transient waveform evaluation (PULSE, SIN, PWL, etc.) so that time-dependent sources drive the circuit during .tran.
acceptanceCriteria: PULSE waveform: v1, v2, td, tr, tf, pw, per — all parameters
SIN waveform: v0, va, freq, td, theta, phi
PWL waveform: piecewise linear interpolation between time-value pairs
Test: PULSE source into RC circuit produces output matching ngspice res_simple.cir transient output
Test: SIN source output samples match analytical sin() within 1e-6

userStories.title: Adaptive timestep control
userStories.description: As a developer, I need adaptive timestep control so that transient analysis is accurate and efficient.
acceptanceCriteria: Local truncation error (LTE) estimation using the difference between BE and trap results
Timestep adjustment: reduce if LTE > RELTOL, increase if LTE << RELTOL
Breakpoint handling: force timestep at source discontinuities (PULSE edges, PWL corners)
Test: PULSE into RC — adaptive stepping places steps near edges, coasts during flat regions
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/transient/fourbitadder.cir as integration test (can be #[ignore] until BJT model exists)

userStories.title: AC analysis (.ac)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AC small-signal analysis so that frequency response can be computed.
acceptanceCriteria: Linearize circuit at DC operating point
Sweep frequency: DEC, OCT, LIN modes
AC source magnitude and phase applied correctly
Results: complex node voltages at each frequency point
Test: RC lowpass filter, -3dB point at f = 1/(2*pi*RC) within 1%
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/filters/ test as integration test

userStories.title: BJT (bipolar junction transistor) model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a BJT device model so that bipolar transistor circuits can be simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: Ebers-Moll model (level 1, matching ngspice default BJT)
.model parameters: BF, BR, IS, VAF, VAR, IKF, IKR, ISE, ISC, NE, NC, RB, RC, RE, CJE, CJC, CJS, TF, TR
NR stamps for collector and base currents
Test: common-emitter amplifier DC bias point matches ngspice within 1e-6
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/general/ tests that use BJTs
userStories.notes: Ebers-Moll/Gummel-Poon level 1 BJT with NPN/PNP, Early effect, high-injection rolloff, series resistances, junction caps. Integrated into OP, DC sweep, AC, and transient analyses.

userStories.title: MOSFET level 1 model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a basic MOSFET model so that MOS circuits can be simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: .model parameters: VTO, KP, LAMBDA, PHI, GAMMA, CBD, CBS, CGSO, CGDO, CGBO
Operating regions: cutoff, linear, saturation with correct equations
Test: NMOS inverter transfer curve matches ngspice within 1e-6
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/mos6/ tests (or simplest MOSFET tests available)

userStories.title: JFET model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a JFET device model.
acceptanceCriteria: Standard JFET model matching ngspice level 1
.model parameters: VTO, BETA, LAMBDA, RD, RS, CGS, CGD, IS
Test: JFET amplifier bias matches ngspice

userStories.title: Subcircuit expansion (.subckt / X)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need subcircuit instantiation so that hierarchical circuits can be flattened and simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: Flatten nested .subckt definitions into a flat netlist before MNA assembly
Port mapping: subcircuit ports connected to caller nets
PARAMS: parameter substitution in subcircuit instances
Nested subcircuits (subcircuit containing another subcircuit call)
Test: voltage divider as subcircuit, instantiated and simulated, matches direct circuit

userStories.title: Controlled sources (E, F, G, H)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need linear controlled sources so that dependent sources can be simulated.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: op-amp modeled as high-gain VCVS, inverting amplifier gain = -R2/R1
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/general/ tests that use controlled sources

userStories.title: Noise analysis (.noise)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need noise analysis to compute spectral noise density.
acceptanceCriteria: Output: noise spectral density at each frequency point
Test: resistor thermal noise matches 4kTR analytically

userStories.title: Transfer function (.tf) and sensitivity (.sens) analyses
userStories.description: As a developer, I need .tf and .sens analyses.
acceptanceCriteria: .tf output input computes small-signal transfer function, input/output resistance
.sens output computes sensitivity of output to each component

userStories.title: Pole-zero analysis (.pz)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pole-zero analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Compute poles and zeros of the transfer function

userStories.title: BSIM3 MOSFET model — parameter parsing and temperature preprocessing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need BSIM3v3 parameter definitions and temperature-dependent preprocessing so that the model infrastructure is ready for the DC equations.
acceptanceCriteria: Bsim3Model struct with all ~116 BSIM3v3 parameters (port from ngspice b3mpar.c / bsim3def.h)
from_model_def() parser that reads .model NMOS/PMOS LEVEL=8 (or LEVEL=49) parameters
Default values matching ngspice for every parameter
Temperature preprocessing: effective mobility, threshold voltage adjustments (port b3temp.c)
Bsim3Instance struct with per-instance geometry (W, L, NF, etc.) and resolved effective params
NMOS and PMOS type handling with correct sign conventions
Unit tests: parse a BSIM3 .model card, verify all defaults, verify temperature scaling
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice-upstream/src/spicelib/devices/bsim3v32/ — b3mpar.c (1786 lines), b3temp.c (894 lines), bsim3def.h (1955 lines). Focus on data structures and preprocessing only, no DC/AC equations yet.

userStories.title: BSIM3 MOSFET model — DC equations and NR stamps
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM3v3 DC load function so that BSIM3 transistors can be solved at operating point.
acceptanceCriteria: DC load function porting the core of b3ld.c: threshold voltage (Vth), drain current (Ids), output conductance (gds), transconductance (gm, gmbs)
Short-channel effects: DIBL, channel-length modulation, velocity saturation, CLM
Companion model (linearized conductance + current source) for Newton-Raphson iteration
stamp_bsim3() function following the same pattern as stamp_mosfet()
Integration into simulate_op, simulate_dc (add to has_nonlinear check, NR load closure)
Test: NMOS DC sweep (Vgs sweep at fixed Vds) — Ids curve shape matches ngspice within 1%
Test: PMOS operating point matches ngspice within 1e-6
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice b3ld.c (3191 lines) — the core DC equations are ~1500 lines, rest is charge/cap. Focus on DC only; AC/charge model is next story.

userStories.title: BSIM3 MOSFET model — AC, charge model, and test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM3 charge/capacitance model and AC stamps so that AC and transient analysis work with BSIM3.
acceptanceCriteria: Charge model: Qg, Qd, Qs, Qb gate charge partitioning (capmod 0/1/2/3)
Parasitic capacitances: overlap caps, junction caps with bias dependence
AC small-signal stamp: gm, gds, gmbs conductances + capacitance matrix
Transient companion model for charge storage elements
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/bsim3/ qaSpec tests as integration tests
All BSIM3 tests pass (DC sweep, AC frequency response, noise if applicable)
userStories.notes: Reference: second half of b3ld.c (charge equations), b3acld.c. The qaSpec tests use a parametric format — may need a simple qaSpec parser or manual conversion to .cir files.

userStories.title: BSIM4 MOSFET model — parameter parsing and temperature preprocessing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need BSIM4 parameter definitions and temperature preprocessing, building on BSIM3 infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Bsim4Model struct with all ~212 BSIM4 parameters (port from ngspice b4mpar.c / bsim4def.h)
from_model_def() parser that reads .model NMOS/PMOS LEVEL=14 (or LEVEL=54) parameters
Default values matching ngspice for every parameter
Bsim4Instance struct with per-instance geometry and effective parameters
New BSIM4-specific features: well-proximity effect (wpemod), geometry-dependent parasitics (geomod)
Unit tests: parse a BSIM4 .model card, verify defaults, verify temperature scaling
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice-upstream/src/spicelib/devices/bsim4/ — b4mpar.c (3685 lines), b4temp.c (2408 lines), bsim4def.h (3931 lines). BSIM4 has ~83% more parameters than BSIM3. Focus on data structures only.

userStories.title: BSIM4 MOSFET model — DC equations and NR stamps
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM4 DC load function so that BSIM4 transistors can be solved.
acceptanceCriteria: DC load function porting b4ld.c: threshold voltage, drain current, conductances
BSIM4-specific effects: GIDL/GISL (gate-induced drain/source leakage), new mobility models
Companion model and stamp_bsim4() for NR iteration
Test: NMOS DC sweep matches ngspice within 1%
Test: PMOS operating point matches ngspice within 1e-6
userStories.notes: Reference: b4ld.c (5623 lines). The DC core is ~2500 lines, rest is charge model. Can reuse patterns from BSIM3 DC implementation.

userStories.title: BSIM4 MOSFET model — AC, charge model, noise, and test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM4 charge, noise, and AC models and all tests passing.
acceptanceCriteria: Charge model: Qg, Qd, Qs, Qb with BSIM4 charge partitioning (capmod options)
Noise model: thermal noise (tnoiMod 0/1/2), flicker noise (fnoiMod 0/1), induced gate noise
AC small-signal stamps and transient companion model
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/bsim4/ qaSpec tests as integration tests
userStories.notes: Reference: b4noi.c for noise, second half of b4ld.c for charges. BSIM4 has more sophisticated noise than BSIM3 (tnoimod, fnoimod combinations).

userStories.title: BSIM3-SOI Partially Depleted model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM3SOI-PD model for silicon-on-insulator circuits.
acceptanceCriteria: BSIM3SOI-PD model implementation (port from ngspice b3soipdld.c and related files)
SOI-specific physics: floating body, parasitic bipolar, self-heating
DC load, AC stamps, and transient companion
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice bsim3soipd/ — 15,789 lines total. Can leverage BSIM3 core equations + SOI extensions. Largest SOI variant.

userStories.title: BSIM3-SOI Fully Depleted model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM3SOI-FD model.
acceptanceCriteria: Front-channel dominant physics, simpler body model than PD
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice bsim3soifd/ — 13,884 lines. Should share significant infrastructure with BSIM3SOI-PD.

userStories.title: BSIM3-SOI Double Diffused model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BSIM3SOI-DD model.
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice bsim3soidd/ — 14,860 lines. Third SOI variant, should reuse most of PD/FD infrastructure.

userStories.title: VBIC bipolar model — parameters and DC equations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the VBIC (Vertical Bipolar Inter-Company) advanced BJT model for DC analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: VbicModel struct with all parameters (port from ngspice vbicmpar.c / vbicdefs.h)
DC load function: forward/reverse transport currents, parasitic base current paths, avalanche multiplication
Test: VBIC CE amplifier DC bias matches ngspice within 1e-6
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice vbic/ — 9,742 lines total. vbicload.c (4118 lines) has the core equations. 108-element parameter array per instance.

userStories.title: VBIC bipolar model — AC, charge model, and test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the VBIC AC/charge model and all 6 tests passing.
acceptanceCriteria: Junction charge models for all VBIC capacitances
Port ngspice-upstream/tests/vbic/ tests (6 tests: CEamp, CBamp, CCamp, and variants)
userStories.notes: Builds on US-030 DC implementation. The charge model adds junction capacitances and transit time effects.

userStories.title: MESA FET model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MESA (Metal Semiconductor) FET model.
acceptanceCriteria: MESA FET model implementation (port from ngspice mesa/ — 3,570 lines)
userStories.notes: MESA is a MESFET variant. Reference: ngspice mesa/ — 3,570 lines total, moderate complexity.

userStories.title: MOS6 MOSFET model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MOS level 6 model.
acceptanceCriteria: MOS6 model implementation (port from ngspice mos6/ — 3,594 lines)
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice mos6/ — 3,594 lines. Can build on existing MOSFET level 1 infrastructure.

userStories.title: HFET and MESFET models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the HFET (heterojunction FET) and basic MESFET models.
acceptanceCriteria: HFET model implementation (port from ngspice hfet/ — 3,304 lines)
MESFET model implementation (port from ngspice mes/ — 3,757 lines)
userStories.notes: Two small related FET models. HFET and MES are both ~3,500 lines each, moderate complexity, related to JFET/MESA family.

userStories.title: Lossy transmission line model (LTRA)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lossy transmission line model for transient simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: LTRA model implementation (port from ngspice ltra/ — 4,660 lines)
R, L, G, C per-unit-length parameters and length
History storage and convolution for transient analysis
userStories.notes: Reference: ngspice ltra/ — 4,660 lines. Key challenge is ltramisc.c (1727 lines) with history/convolution. Most complex transmission line model.

userStories.title: TXL and CPL transmission line models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TXL (single) and CPL (coupled multiconductor) transmission line models.
acceptanceCriteria: TXL model implementation (port from ngspice txl/ — 2,567 lines)
CPL model implementation (port from ngspice cpl/ — 4,010 lines)
Port remaining transmission line tests from ngspice-upstream/tests/transmission/
All transmission line tests pass (6 total across LTRA + TXL + CPL)
userStories.notes: TXL is simpler than LTRA. CPL extends TXL to coupled lines. Can share infrastructure with LTRA from US-035.

userStories.title: Regression tests — parser and control flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the parser and control flow regression tests passing.
userStories.notes: These test the netlist parser, function evaluation, and .lib/.include processing. May require parser enhancements in ferrospice-netlist.

userStories.title: Regression tests — models, temperature, and misc
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining regression tests passing.
acceptanceCriteria: Port ngspice-upstream/tests/regression/sens/ and regression/pz/ tests (if not already ported)
userStories.notes: Temperature tests may require .TEMP and .PARAM TEMPER support in the parser. Misc tests cover edge cases across all analyses.

userStories.title: Test harness matching ngspice check.sh
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an automated test harness that runs all 113 .cir files and compares output to .out references.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust integration test or binary that iterates ngspice-upstream/tests/**/*.cir
For each .cir: parse, simulate, format output, filter, diff against .out
Filtering matches check.sh rules (strip metadata, normalize floats)

userStories.title: Minimal CLI for running netlists
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLI to run SPICE netlists so I can use ferrospice from the command line.
acceptanceCriteria: ferrospice --batch input.cir runs all analyses and prints results to stdout
Output format matches ngspice batch output (for test compatibility)
CLI is a thin wrapper around the library — all logic lives in library crates
